verses.latin_vulgate: Deus, Deus meus, ad te de luce vigilo.  Sitivit in te anima mea ; quam multipliciter tibi caro mea !
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֱלֹהִ֤ים ׀ אֵלִ֥י אַתָּ֗ה אֲֽשַׁ֫חֲרֶ֥ךָּ צָמְאָ֬ה לְךָ֨ ׀ נַפְשִׁ֗י כָּמַ֣הּ לְךָ֣ בְשָׂרִ֑י בְּאֶרֶץ־צִיָּ֖ה וְעָיֵ֣ף בְּלִי־מָֽיִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: O God, my God, to thee do I watch at break of day. For thee my soul hath thirsted; for thee my flesh, O how many ways!
verses.finnish_biblia: Davidin Psalmi, kuin hän oli Juudan korvessa. (H63:2) Jumala, sinä olet minun Jumalani! Varhain minä sinua etsin: sinua minun sieluni janoo, minun lihani halaa sinua karkiassa ja kuivassa maassa, joka vedetöin on.
verses.king_james: O God, thou art my God; early will I seek thee: my soul thirsteth for thee, my flesh longeth for thee in a dry and thirsty land, where no water is;

verses.latin_vulgate: In terra deserta, et invia, et inaquosa,  sic in sancto apparui tibi,  ut viderem virtutem tuam et gloriam tuam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֵּ֭ן בַּקֹּ֣דֶשׁ חֲזִיתִ֑ךָ לִרְא֥וֹת עֻ֝זְּךָ֗ וּכְבוֹדֶֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: In a desert land, and where there is no way, and no water: so in the sanctuary have I come before thee, to see thy power and thy glory.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niinkuin minä näin sinun pyhässä, katsellakseni sinun voimaas ja kunniaas;
verses.king_james: To see thy power and thy glory, so as I have seen thee in the sanctuary.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quoniam melior est misericordia tua super vitas,  labia mea laudabunt te.
verses.douay_rheims: For thy mercy is better than lives: thee my lips will praise.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä sinun laupiutes on parempi kuin elämä: minun huuleni pitää sinua kiittämän.
verses.king_james: Because thy lovingkindness is better than life, my lips shall praise thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sic benedicam te in vita mea,  et in nomine tuo levabo manus meas.
verses.douay_rheims: Thus will I bless thee all my life long: and in thy name I will lift up my hands.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin minä tahdon kunnioittaa sinua minun elinaikanani, ja minun käteni nostaa ylös sinun nimees.
verses.king_james: Thus will I bless thee while I live: I will lift up my hands in thy name.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut adipe et pinguedine repleatur anima mea,  et labiis exsultationis laudabit os meum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּמ֤וֹ חֵ֣לֶב וָ֭דֶשֶׁן תִּשְׂבַּ֣ע נַפְשִׁ֑י וְשִׂפְתֵ֥י רְ֝נָנ֗וֹת יְהַלֶּל־פִּֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Let my soul be filled as with marrow and fatness: and my mouth shall praise thee with joyful lips.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niinkuin lihavuudella ja rasvalla pitää minun sieluni ravittaman: ja minun suuni pitää kiittämän iloisilla huulilla.
verses.king_james: My soul shall be satisfied as with marrow and fatness; and my mouth shall praise thee with joyful lips:

verses.latin_vulgate: Si memor fui tui super stratum meum,  in matutinis meditabor in te.
verses.douay_rheims: If I have remembered thee upon my bed, I will meditate on thee in the morning:
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin minä vuoteeseni lasken, niin minä muistan sinua:kuin minä herään, niin minä puhun sinusta.
verses.king_james: When I remember thee upon my bed, and meditate on thee in the night watches.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia fuisti adjutor meus,  et in velamento alarum tuarum exsultabo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־הָיִ֣יתָ עֶזְרָ֣תָה לִּ֑י וּבְצֵ֖ל כְּנָפֶ֣יךָ אֲרַנֵּֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: Because thou hast been my helper. And I will rejoice under the cover of thy wings:
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä sinä olet minun apuni, ja sinun siipeis varjon alla minä kerskaan.
verses.king_james: Because thou hast been my help, therefore in the shadow of thy wings will I rejoice.

verses.latin_vulgate: Adhæsit anima mea post te ;  me suscepit dextera tua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דָּבְקָ֣ה נַפְשִׁ֣י אַחֲרֶ֑יךָ בִּ֝֗י תָּמְכָ֥ה יְמִינֶֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: My soul hath stuck close to thee: thy right hand hath received me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun sieluni riippuu sinussa: sinun oikia kätes minun tukee.
verses.king_james: My soul followeth hard after thee: thy right hand upholdeth me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ipsi vero in vanum quæsierunt animam meam :  introibunt in inferiora terræ ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהֵ֗מָּה לְ֭שׁוֹאָה יְבַקְשׁ֣וּ נַפְשִׁ֑י יָ֝בֹ֗אוּ בְּֽתַחְתִּיּ֥וֹת הָאָֽרֶץ׃
verses.douay_rheims: But they have sought my soul in vain, they shall go into the lower parts of the earth:
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta he etsivät kadottaaksensa minun sieluni: heidän täytyy maan alle mennä.
verses.king_james: But those that seek my soul, to destroy it, shall go into the lower parts of the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: tradentur in manus gladii :  partes vulpium erunt.
verses.douay_rheims: They shall be delivered into the hands of the sword, they shall be the portions of foxes.
verses.finnish_biblia: Heidän pitää miekkaan lankeeman, ketuille osaksi tuleman.
verses.king_james: They shall fall by the sword: they shall be a portion for foxes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Rex vero lætabitur in Deo ;  laudabuntur omnes qui jurant in eo :  quia obstructum est os loquentium iniqua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהַמֶּלֶךְ֮ יִשְׂמַ֢ח בֵּאלֹ֫הִ֥ים יִ֭תְהַלֵּל כׇּל־הַנִּשְׁבָּ֣ע בּ֑וֹ כִּ֥י יִ֝סָּכֵ֗ר פִּ֣י דוֹבְרֵי־שָֽׁקֶר׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: But the king shall rejoice in God, all they shall be praised that swear by him: because the mouth is stopped of them that speak wicked things.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta kuninkaan pitää iloitseman Jumalassa: joka hänen kauttansa vannoo, se kunnioitetaan; sillä valhetteliain suu pitää tukittaman.
verses.king_james: But the king shall rejoice in God; every one that sweareth by him shall glory: but the mouth of them that speak lies shall be stopped.
